  • Download Rmd

Functional beta diveristy analysis

This is an R Markdown Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the Run button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing Ctrl+Shift+Enter.

PCoA Plots based on pathway-based Bray-Curtis Distance


library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ape)


# Read normalized pathway abundance table
path_abun_table <- file.path(
  "../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func/Normalized_Tables",
  "merged_pathabundance_relab.tsv"
)

path_abun <- read.table(
  path_abun_table,
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  comment.char = "",
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Rename the first column as "Pathway"
colnames(path_abun)[1] <- "Pathway"

# Remove UNMAPPED and UNINTEGRATED rows
select_path_abun <- path_abun %>%
  filter(!Pathway %in% c("UNMAPPED", "UNINTEGRATED"))

# Remove taxonomically stratified pathway rows
select_path_abun <- select_path_abun %>%
  filter(!grepl("\\|", Pathway))

cat("Number of pathways after removing UNMAPPED, UNINTEGRATED, and stratified rows:",
    nrow(select_path_abun), "\n")
Number of pathways after removing UNMAPPED, UNINTEGRATED, and stratified rows: 321 
# Remove samples that should be excluded
select_path_abun <- select_path_abun %>%
  select(-NW046_5_all_merged_Abundance)

cat("Number of columns after sample removal:", ncol(select_path_abun), "\n")
Number of columns after sample removal: 49 
# Convert to matrix: rows = pathways, columns = samples
mat_path <- select_path_abun %>%
  column_to_rownames("Pathway") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Ensure all values are numeric
storage.mode(mat_path) <- "numeric"

# Transpose to samples x pathways
mat_rel <- t(mat_path)

# Calculate pathway prevalence across samples
prev <- colMeans(mat_rel > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

# Filter pathways by prevalence only
prev_cut <- 0.10
keep <- prev >= prev_cut

mat_rel_filt <- mat_rel[, keep, drop = FALSE]

cat("Number of pathways retained after prevalence filtering:",
    ncol(mat_rel_filt), "\n")
Number of pathways retained after prevalence filtering: 285 
# Calculate Bray-Curtis distance
dist_bc <- vegdist(mat_rel_filt, method = "bray")

###### Analysis based on Bray-Curtis distance

### PCoA
pcoa_res <- ape::pcoa(dist_bc)

pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_res$vectors[, 1:2])
colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
pcoa_df$SampleID_in_table <- rownames(pcoa_df)

# Calculate percentage of explained variation using positive eigenvalues only
pcoa1_lab <- paste0("PCoA1 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 1), "%)")
pcoa2_lab <- paste0("PCoA2 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 1), "%)")

### Read metadata
meta_file <- file.path("../../data/metadata", "selected_northwood_map.txt")
metadata <- read_tsv(meta_file, col_types = cols())

# Extract sample prefix from metadata, e.g. "#SampleID" = "NW001-1" -> "NW001"
metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("-.*$", "", `#SampleID`))

# Extract sample prefix from pathway table column names,
# e.g. "NW001_3_all_merged_Abundance" -> "NW001"
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", SampleID_in_table)) %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_prefix")

# Create simplified sample labels for plotting
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Update_SampleID = sub("_all_merged.*$", "", SampleID_in_table))

filtered_pcoa_df <- pcoa_df

### PCoA plot colored by FIScore
p_pcoa <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FIScore)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = Update_SampleID),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = 20
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "plasma",
    limits = c(0.3, 0.8),
    oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

p_pcoa


### Save the figure
# figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Func_Pathways_PCoA_Plot_BC_distance.jpg")
# ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = p_pcoa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

PCoA Plots based on pathway-based Aitchison Distance

### Read normalized pathway abundance table and Metadata 
### as described in the previous section

library(compositions)

### Calculate the Aitchison distance
# add a small pseudocount
pseudo <- 1e-6
mat_rel_pc <- mat_rel_filt + pseudo
# re-close to composition (optional but recommended)
mat_rel_pc <- mat_rel_pc / rowSums(mat_rel_pc)
# CLR transform
mat_clr <- compositions::clr(mat_rel_pc)
# Aitchison distance = Euclidean distance on CLR data
dist_aitchison <- dist(mat_clr, method = "euclidean")


###### Analysis based on Aitchison distance
### PCoA
pcoa_res <- ape::pcoa(dist_aitchison)

pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_res$vectors[, 1:2])
colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
pcoa_df$SampleID_in_table <- rownames(pcoa_df)

# Calculate percentage of explained variation using positive eigenvalues only
pcoa1_lab <- paste0("PCoA1 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 1), "%)")
pcoa2_lab <- paste0("PCoA2 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 1), "%)")


# Extract sample prefix from pathway table column names,
# e.g. "NW001_3_all_merged_Abundance" -> "NW001"
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", SampleID_in_table)) %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_prefix")

# Create simplified sample labels for plotting
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Update_SampleID = sub("_all_merged.*$", "", SampleID_in_table))

filtered_pcoa_df <- pcoa_df

### PCoA plot colored by FIScore
p_pcoa <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FIScore)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = Update_SampleID),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = 20
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "plasma",
    limits = c(0.3, 0.8),
    oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Aitchison)",
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

p_pcoa



# figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Func_Pathways_PCoA_Plot_AT_distance.jpg")
# ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = p_pcoa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

PCoA Plots based on gene family-based Bray-Curtis Distance

library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(ape)


# Read CPM-normalized gene family abundance table
gf_abun_table <- file.path(
  "../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func/Normalized_Tables",
  "merged_genefamilies_cpm.tsv"
)

gf_abun <- read.table(
  gf_abun_table,
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  comment.char = "",
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Rename the first column as "Gene_Family"
colnames(gf_abun)[1] <- "Gene_Family"

# Remove UNMAPPED and UNINTEGRATED rows
select_gf_abun <- gf_abun %>%
  filter(!Gene_Family %in% c("UNMAPPED", "UNINTEGRATED"))

# Remove taxonomically stratified Gene_Family rows
select_gf_abun <- select_gf_abun %>%
  filter(!grepl("\\|", Gene_Family))

cat("Number of Gene Families after removing UNMAPPED, UNINTEGRATED, and stratified rows:",
    nrow(select_gf_abun), "\n")
Number of Gene Families after removing UNMAPPED, UNINTEGRATED, and stratified rows: 1570359 
# Remove samples that should be excluded
select_gf_abun <- select_gf_abun %>% 
  select(-`NW046_5_all_merged_Abundance-RPKs`)

cat("Number of columns after sample removal:", ncol(select_gf_abun), "\n")
Number of columns after sample removal: 49 
# Convert to matrix: rows = Gene Families, columns = samples
mat_gf <- select_gf_abun %>%
  column_to_rownames("Gene_Family") %>%
  as.matrix()

# Ensure all values are numeric
storage.mode(mat_gf) <- "numeric"

# Transpose to samples x Gene Families
mat_rel <- t(mat_gf)

# Calculate Gene_Family prevalence across samples
prev <- colMeans(mat_rel > 0, na.rm = TRUE)

# Filter Gene Families by prevalence only
prev_cut <- 0.10
keep <- prev >= prev_cut

mat_rel_filt <- mat_rel[, keep, drop = FALSE]

cat("Number of Gene Families retained after prevalence filtering:",
    ncol(mat_rel_filt), "\n")
Number of Gene Families retained after prevalence filtering: 498679 
# Calculate Bray-Curtis distance
dist_bc <- vegdist(mat_rel_filt, method = "bray")

###### Analysis based on Bray-Curtis distance

### PCoA
pcoa_res <- pcoa(dist_bc)

pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_res$vectors[, 1:2])
colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
pcoa_df$SampleID_in_table <- rownames(pcoa_df)

# Calculate percentage of explained variation using positive eigenvalues only
pcoa1_lab <- paste0("PCoA1 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 1), "%)")
pcoa2_lab <- paste0("PCoA2 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 1), "%)")

### Read metadata
meta_file <- file.path("../../data/metadata", "selected_northwood_map.txt")
metadata <- read_tsv(meta_file, col_types = cols())

# Extract sample prefix from metadata, e.g. "#SampleID" = "NW001-1" -> "NW001"
metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("-.*$", "", `#SampleID`))

# Extract sample prefix from Gene_Family table column names,
# e.g. "NW001_3_all_merged_Abundance-RPKs" -> "NW001"
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", SampleID_in_table)) %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_prefix")

# Create simplified sample labels for plotting
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Update_SampleID = sub("_all_merged.*$", "", SampleID_in_table))

filtered_pcoa_df <- pcoa_df

### PCoA plot colored by FIScore
p_pcoa <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FIScore)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = Update_SampleID),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = 20
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "plasma",
    limits = c(0.3, 0.8),
    oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of gene family abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

p_pcoa


### Save the figure
# figure_file <- file.path("../../figures/PCoA_Plots", "Func_GeneFamily_PCoA_BC_distance.jpg")
# ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = p_pcoa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

PCoA Plots based on gene family-based Aitchison Distance

### Read normalized pathway abundance table and Metadata 
### as described in the previous section

library(compositions)

### Calculate the Aitchison distance
# add a small pseudocount
pseudo <- 1e-6
mat_rel_pc <- mat_rel_filt + pseudo
# re-close to composition (optional but recommended)
mat_rel_pc <- mat_rel_pc / rowSums(mat_rel_pc)
# CLR transform
mat_clr <- compositions::clr(mat_rel_pc)
# Aitchison distance = Euclidean distance on CLR data
dist_aitchison <- dist(mat_clr, method = "euclidean")


###### Analysis based on Aitchison distance
### PCoA
pcoa_res <- ape::pcoa(dist_aitchison)

pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_res$vectors[, 1:2])
colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
pcoa_df$SampleID_in_table <- rownames(pcoa_df)

# Calculate percentage of explained variation using positive eigenvalues only
pcoa1_lab <- paste0("PCoA1 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 1), "%)")
pcoa2_lab <- paste0("PCoA2 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 1), "%)")


# Extract sample prefix from gene family table column names,
# e.g. "NW001_3_all_merged_Abundance-RPKs" -> "NW001"
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", SampleID_in_table)) %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_prefix")

# Create simplified sample labels for plotting
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Update_SampleID = sub("_all_merged.*$", "", SampleID_in_table))

filtered_pcoa_df <- pcoa_df

### PCoA plot colored by FIScore
p_pcoa <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FIScore)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = Update_SampleID),
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = 20
  ) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = "plasma",
    limits = c(0.3, 0.8),
    oob = scales::squish
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of gene family abundance (Aitchison)",
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

p_pcoa


# figure_file <- file.path("../../figures/PCoA_Plots", "Func_GeneFamily_PCoA_AT_distance.jpg")
# ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = p_pcoa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

Add a new chunk by clicking the Insert Chunk button on the toolbar or by pressing Ctrl+Alt+I.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the Preview button or press Ctrl+Shift+K to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike Knit, Preview does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
